title: 怪屋
summary:
Brenda Lionides collapses in terror, claiming she is being trapped and denying any involvement in the crime. Taverner informs her that Lawrence Brown has also been arrested. Brenda, visibly devastated, is led to a police car by Taverner, accompanied by Mr. Lawrence Brown and Sheriff Lamb. Charles observes Brenda’s expression of absolute fear, which reminds him of a story Madge Leoniadis once told about Edith Thompson. Charles speculates that Lawrence Brown might have swapped the contents of poison bottles to protect Brenda, given his unstable personality and delusions of persecution.

Charles explains to Sophia that the police arrested them based on evidence found in love letters. During their conversation, Charles suggests they should marry now that the Leonidis family is no longer under suspicion. However, Sophia reveals she had a motive: her grandfather had told her two weeks before his death that he was leaving her his entire inheritance. She had kept this secret because she feared the will would make her a suspect in the murder.

Later, Roger and Clementine approach the group. Roger expresses aggressive satisfaction regarding the arrests, stating he hopes the criminals are hanged for poisoning his father. Clementine rebukes his rudeness. As they walk toward the house, Clementine confesses to Charles her intense desire to take Roger away from the Leonidis family traditions. She explains that she has been fighting to ensure Roger can live a life of his own choosing, free from the constraints of his family's expectations.
